name: Ed Sullivan
abstract: Edward Vincent Sullivan (September 28, 1901 – October 13, 1974) was an American television host, impresario, sports and entertainment reporter, and syndicated columnist for the New York Daily News and the Chicago Tribune New York News Syndicate. He was the creator and host of the television variety program Toast of the Town, which in 195…

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Pittsburgh Courier Poll Winners Jazz Concert
Midnight Performance: Pittsburgh Courier Poll Winners Jazz Concert

about_quotes.speaker: Rob Mounsey
about_quotes.evidence: when The Beatles broke on Ed Sullivan I just thought oh that stuff is not for me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Rob Mounsey interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 9, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Catherine Russell
about_quotes.evidence: Then of course we’d watch a lot of Ed Sullivan, you know, on Sunday nights.
about_quotes.doc_title: Catherine Russell interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, October 26, 2018

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Morgenstern
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee mentions Ed Sullivan only in the context of a television show ('Ed Sullivan') where the Jazz and People's Movement staged a sit-in. There is no indication of a personal relationship, meeting, or direct collaboration between Dan Morgenstern and Ed Sullivan.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dan Morgenstern — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Paul Shaffer
about_quotes.evidence: Ed had everybody that was anybody in show business and he made stars at that time, and we could see it on Canadian television, the CBC carried “The Ed Sullivan Show.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Paul Shaffer interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, August 24, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Nat Hentoff
about_quotes.evidence: there was practically nothing on television except for the Timex All Stars, or Ed Sullivan, where Duke would get to play two of his standards.
about_quotes.doc_title: Nat Hentoff interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Luther Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: I would work for him... my work with Ed Sullivan shows would normally consist of playing and conducting... for the star whom I was working for
about_quotes.doc_title: Luther Henderson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
